feature: Add a small footer note saying "Powered by Agentic Dev Platform"
plan:
# Implementation Plan ## Summary Add a small footer note reading **"Powered by Agentic Dev Platform"**. The app already has a shared `components/Footer.tsx` rendered globally via `app/layout.tsx`, so a single, minimal addition to the existing footer markup covers every page. The API routes listed under "Affected Files" are not relevant to a UI footer change and should be ignored. ## Files To Inspect - `components/Footer.tsx` — the existing footer; this is the only file that needs editing. - `app/layout.tsx` — confirms `
` is rendered once for all pages (no per-page changes needed). - `lib/version.js` — existing styling/value pattern for footer items (for stylistic consistency only). ## Implementation Steps 1. In `components/Footer.tsx`, add a new note inside the existing `
`, following the established separator + `` pattern. Append after the last item (the build span block): ```tsx · Powered by Agentic Dev Platform ``` 2. Keep the muted styling consistent with the surrounding `text-xs text-slate-400` footer. No new imports, props, or state required. 3. No changes to `app/layout.tsx` — the footer is already mounted globally. ## Validation Steps 1. Run the dev server (`npm run dev`) and load any page (e.g. `/`, `/reports`, `/dashboard`); confirm the note appears centered in the footer alongside the existing version/env/build items. 2. Verify the separator dot renders correctly and wrapping behaves on narrow widths (the footer uses `flex-wrap`). 3. Typecheck/build (`npm run build` or `tsc`) to confirm no JSX/markup errors. ## Risks - **Very low risk.** Purely additive, presentational change in one shared component. - The listed "Affected Files" (API routes) are misleading — editing them is unnecessary and would be out of scope; restrict the change to `Footer.tsx`. - Minor: if Russian localization is expected (the app uses `lang="ru"`), confirm whether the note should stay in English ("Agentic Dev Platform" is a proper name, so English is reasonable) — otherwise no localization work needed.
